name: we-are-displaced
description: Malala Yousafzai's intimate account of displacement — her own story of fleeing the Taliban in Pakistan's Swat Valley, told alongside the voices of refugee girls from Yemen, Syria, Iraq, Colombia, Guatemala, Congo, and Myanmar.
useCase: Understanding the refugee experience, resilience after forced displacement, fighting for education under oppression, building a new life in a foreign country, and finding hope in the midst of crisis.
knownRisks: Contains firsthand accounts of violence, death of family members, sexual assault references, and extreme trauma
Some names and identifying details have been changed to protect the individuals
May be emotionally triggering for readers with related personal experiences
The described situations continue to evolve; statistics are from 2017-2019 context
